_source: Role split and the standard 12-man target are paraphrased from WANG Chenhua's community guide (Builds and Company Composition, v1.4): https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1790093811 . It describes a common 12-brother company as 6 frontline + 6 backline, where the backline consists of 2-3 tile melee (polearm) users, a sergeant, and ranged brothers (archer / crossbow / thrower). The spearwall figure comes from the same guide's note that a decent spearwall line usually needs about 4 brothers.
_note: Targets are per ROLE, since the guide gives role-level guidance rather than exact per-archetype counts. The UI shows your actual archetype breakdown inside each role, so you can still spot e.g. too many shield users without the app inventing a precise target for each build.

presets.name: Standard 12 (6 front / 6 back)
presets.note: The guide's common company: 6 frontline and 6 backline, the backline being polearms, a sergeant and ranged brothers.

presets.name: Spearwall + Polearm (12)
presets.note: Built around a spearwall line (~4 shield/spear brothers) with polearms striking over the top — the guide calls this a classic answer to most opponents, including Chosen.

presets.name: Ranged-heavy (12)
presets.note: Leans on archers and crossbows to thin enemies before contact; needs a solid frontline to hold while they fire.

presets.name: Early company (8)
presets.note: A smaller early-game band: mostly cheap frontline bodies with a couple of ranged, before you can afford specialists.
